reviewer_stances.reason: Most specific and substantiated review with the highest confidence (4). Its core criticisms — limited single-task ImageNet evaluation, reliance on an old AlexNet backbone, no clear performance-versus-inference-cost trend, the FLOPs/token vs pixel-count framing, and absent comparisons to token-reduction/efficient-ViT methods (Swin, Pyramid, larger-patch) — are directly adopted by the meta-review, which names its concerns explicitly. Its outlying significance (2) and originality (2) are somewhat harsher than the consensus and the meta-review's positive framing of the novel k-NN convolution, so I did not follow those two scores fully.

Thorough and well-reasoned; the Table-1 factor-mixing/fixation confound is corroborated by HDDR and reflects the meta-review's matched-comparison theme, and the latency/kernel-edge-case/LoRA-sensitivity points are specific though secondary to the meta-review's main thrust. Self-reported confidence is only 2.

Accurate summary and on-target questions — task generalization to COCO detection/ADE20K segmentation and comparison against efficiency/token-reduction methods — that the meta-review echoes. The strengths/weaknesses text is largely descriptive rather than evaluative, so the review's value rests mainly on its questions.

Brief and with generic stated strengths and no real questions, but its two weaknesses — the need for a matched FOVI-ViT-H+ @224 vs Uniform @224 comparison and the missing fovea-inspired baseline — are specific and corroborated by other reviewers, one of which (missing fovea baseline) aligns with HDDR and the meta-review.

score_rationale: I anchored on Reviewer HDDR, whose detailed, high-confidence critique (single-task evaluation, small/old backbones, absent token-reduction comparisons, and unclear efficiency trends) is directly adopted by the meta-review; this pulled significance to a 3 rather than the 4 given by the two briefer reviews, while stopping short of HDDR's 2 given the corroborated value of the contribution. Soundness (3), presentation (3), and originality (3) reflect near-unanimous reviewer agreement and the meta-review's positive framing of the novel k-NN convolution, so I did not follow HDDR's outlying originality 2. Overall recommendation of 4 (weak accept) sits at the mode of the reviewers and matches the meta-review's 'merits acceptance, with revisions' stance. Confidence 4 reflects strongly corroborating criticisms across reviewers (matched-comparison/fixation confounds, missing baselines, task generalization), with only significance genuinely contested.
